channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.description: BULAWAYO residents are urging the city council to introduce separate potable and non-potable water supply systems as part of efforts to conserve treated water, reduce wastage and minimise damage to ro... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Businessman alleges delays in probe involving Mnangagwa nephew @bulawayo24 @PoliceZimbabwe

item.title: ZiG monthly inflation rises to 0.6% @bulawayo24
item.description: THE monthly inflation rate for Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) rose to 0.6% in June 2026 from 0.5% recorded in May, according to the latest figures released by Zimbabwe National Statistics Agency (ZimStat).The la... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Villagers lose US$4,350 in fake nurse aide training scam @bulawayo24

item.title: 72-Hour CAB 3 Ultimatum: Why Zimbabwe’s War Veterans Are Now Turning Against Zanu PF’s New Law... as Mnangagwa Summons Parliament @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Zimbabwe’s Political Quake: War Veterans Issue 72-Hour Ultimatum Over Controversial CAB3 Amidst Bribery Claims Harare – In an unprecedented display of defiance, a formidable group of Zimbabwe’s liberation war veterans has issued a stark 72-hour ultimatum to Parliament, demanding the immediate repeal of the contentious Constitution Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3). This audacious move, coming [...] The post 72-Hour CAB 3 Ultimatum: Why Zimbabwe’s War Veterans Are Now Turning Against Zanu PF’s New Law... as Mnangagwa Summons Parliament first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
